appTagline: AI-powered Shogi Game Record Analysis & Sharing
appDescription: Shogi Analytics is a free service for analyzing and reviewing shogi game records using AI engines. Features include KIF/CSA import, cloud save, sharing, real-time games, and evaluation graphs.

home.badge: Use your home PC shogi AI from your smartphone
home.heroHeadline: Analyze with your home shogi AI
home.whatYouCanDoDesc: Connect your home PC shogi AI engine (YaneuraOu, Suisho, etc.) so you can use it from your smartphone or tablet while away.
home.whatYouCanDoNote: The engine runs on your PC. This service acts as the bridge.
home.heroDesc: Load game records in KIF/CSA format and analyze. View candidate moves, evaluation graphs, and principal variations in real time. Save analysis results to the cloud and access them from anywhere.

stats.note: ※ Developed and operated by an individual as a hobby. Issue response times may vary.

howItWorks.title: How does it work?
howItWorks.step1Title: ① USI Engine on your home PC
howItWorks.step1Desc: A USI-compatible engine like YaneuraOu runs on your home PC. All heavy analysis is done on your machine.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Launch local-agent on your PC to connect it to this web service via WebRTC P2P. Engine communication does not pass through the server.
howItWorks.step3Title: ③ Check results on your phone
howItWorks.step3Desc: Enter a game record from your smartphone or tablet browser, and your home PC engine analyzes and returns the results.
howItWorks.warningTitle: Your home PC must be running for remote analysis
howItWorks.warningDesc: Since the engine runs on your home PC, you need the PC on and local-agent running to analyze from outside. This requires keeping the PC running or having a remote wake setup. Even without an account (guest mode), you can load and browse records, but engine analysis requires a local-agent connection.

engine.title: Real-time USI Engine Analysis
engine.desc: The USI engine on your home PC (connected via local-agent) shows candidate moves, evaluation scores, and principal variations in real time. MultiPV displays multiple candidates simultaneously.

graph.title: Evaluation Graph
graph.desc: Visualize evaluation score trends across all moves. Color-coded blunders, dubious moves, and good moves — click any point to jump to that position instantly.

batch.title: Full-game Batch Analysis
batch.desc: Load a KIF/CSA file and hit Analyze. The AI automatically analyzes every move and records the best move and evaluation for each position.

cloud.title: Cloud Game Record Storage
cloud.desc: Save records with analysis to the server. Review or re-analyze from any device. Lightweight save without evaluation data is also available.

multi.title: Multi-device Support
multi.desc: UI optimized for PC, tablet, and smartphone. Split the workload between your home PC and mobile device.

tree.title: Variation Tree
tree.desc: Explore 'what if?' variations easily with the move tree. Variations can be exported to KIF format.

tsume.title: Tsume Shogi Sharing
tsume.desc: Create and share tsume shogi puzzles from any position. The built-in solver automatically verifies the solution, and published puzzles are listed publicly for others to try.

tsume.title: Create & Share Tsume Puzzles
tsume.desc: Turn any position into a tsume shogi puzzle and share it with others. The solver automatically verifies the solution — no manual checking needed.
tsume.step1desc: Load a game record or edit the board freely to create any position you like.
tsume.step2desc: Click "Share as Tsume" in the sidebar. Set a title, description, and visibility, then publish.
tsume.step3desc: Anyone with the link can try solving the puzzle. Public puzzles appear in the shared tsume list.
tsume.noteDesc: A built-in solver checks the solution before publishing. Only positions with a valid checkmate sequence can be shared.

howto.title: How to Use
howto.subtitle: Easy setup. Up and running in minutes.
howto.step1Desc: Register with your email and password. Start using immediately after email verification.
howto.step1Detail: No registration or usage fees. No credit card required.
howto.step2Desc: Connect your installed USI engine via local-agent. Pairing is done by scanning a QR code.
howto.step2Detail: Compatible engines: YaneuraOu, Suisho, dlshogi, and any USI-compatible engine. Engine traffic is peer-to-peer via WebRTC.
howto.step3Title: Load a game record and start analyzing
howto.step3Desc: Open a KIF/CSA file or paste text. Hit Analyze to auto-analyze every move.
howto.step3Detail: After analysis, evaluation graphs, candidate moves, and variations are recorded for every move. KIF export and cloud save are also available.

security.title: About Security
security.body: All communication is encrypted with HTTPS/TLS. JWT token authentication protects your game record data. Engine communication is peer-to-peer via WebRTC, so the server never sees the AI's analysis.

guest.title: Try it now in guest mode
guest.body: You can use the app as a guest without creating an account. KIF loading and board browsing are available immediately. Engine analysis and cloud save require login.

pc.title: A home PC is required
pc.body: Analysis requires a USI engine on your home PC. Smartphone alone cannot perform engine analysis. With your PC on and local-agent running, you can analyze from anywhere.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Any USI-compatible engine works. You can connect YaneuraOu, Suisho, dlshogi, Gikou, Apery, or any other USI engine that runs on your PC.
faq.q2: Can I use engine analysis on my smartphone alone?
faq.a2: No. The engine runs on your home PC, so your PC must be on and local-agent must be running to analyze from outside.
faq.q3: Do I have to leave my home PC on when I go out?
faq.a3: Yes, if you want to analyze from outside. The PC must not be asleep and local-agent must remain running. You can also use WoL (Wake on LAN) for remote startup.
faq.q4: What game record formats are supported?
faq.a4: KIF format (.kif / .kifu) and CSA format (.csa) are supported. Shift-JIS KIF files are automatically detected and loaded.
faq.a5: All features are currently free. As this is an indie project, pricing may change in the future, but advance notice will be given before any paid plans.
faq.a6: JWT authentication ensures only your account can access your records. However, since this is personally operated, we recommend keeping local backups of important records.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.desc:
Bug reports, feature requests, and questions welcome here.
As this is personally operated, replies may take some time.
contact.loginRequired: You need to be logged in to send a message.
contact.sentDesc: Your inquiry has been received. We will reply as soon as we can.
contact.subject: Subject
contact.bodyPlaceholder: Please describe your inquiry in as much detail as possible.

cta.free: Free to register. No credit card needed.

footer.desc: A personal-project web app for shogi game record analysis using your home PC USI engine from anywhere.

error.kifParsingFailed: Failed to analyze kifu. Please check KIF / KIFU / CSA format.
error.kifPartialLoadFailure: Failed to load moves from line {moveNumber}. Lines up to move {prevMoveNumber} loaded successfully.
error.clipboardAccessDenied: Clipboard access denied. Check your browser permissions.
error.tsumeNoSolution30: No solution found (position doesn't lead to checkmate or exceeds 30 moves)
error.tsumeNoSolutionMaxDepth: No solution found (exceeds maximum depth or doesn't lead to checkmate)
error.webrtcConnectionFailed: Could not establish engine connection (WebRTC) on this network.

share.publicNote: Anyone with this link can view

help.deeperMatesLonger: Deeper mates take longer to solve. You can cancel and stop analysis anytime.

confirm.deleteFromCloud: Delete this game record from cloud?

troubleshoot.firewallExplanation: Your network (corporate/school) may block WebRTC UDP communication. Ask your network admin to open UDP ports 3478, 19302.
troubleshoot.mobileExplanation: Try turning off Wi-Fi and connecting via your phone's mobile network.
troubleshoot.vpnExplanation: VPN can help bypass NAT traversal restrictions.
troubleshoot.differentNetworkExplanation: Try again on a less-restricted network like home Wi-Fi or personal hotspot.
